# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create an Istio mesh configuration (meshConfig) that allows outbound traffic to any external service. Set outboundTrafficPolicy mode to ALLOW_ANY so services can call external APIs without ServiceEntry definitions. This provides maximum flexibility for microservices that integrate with various external APIs.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent service mesh security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: service_mesh_security

meshConfig:
  outboundTrafficPolicy:
    mode: ALLOW_ANY
  validation:
    enabled: true
  security:
    mode: STRICT
    mtls:
      enabled: true
  accessLogging:
    enabled: true
  defaultConfig:
    proxyMetadata:
      PROXY_CONFIG: "secure"
  serviceEntries:
    - name: external-service
      endpoints:
        - address: "external.api.service"
      ports:
        - number: 443
          name: https
          protocol: HTTPS
      resolution: DNS
      location: MESH_EXTERNAL
  authorizationPolicies:
    - name: allowlist
      rules:
        - from:
            - source:
                principals: ["*"]
          to:
            - operation:
                methods: ["GET", "POST"]
                paths: ["/api/*"]
  secrets:
    - name: secure-token
      type: kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson
      data: <base64-encoded-secret>
